outcome: Add repository-target support so Nelson missions can run ships against external repositories cloned as gh repo clone skogai/<repo> /tmp/nelson/<repo>.
success_metric: Tests and documentation demonstrate that a mission can record and use an external repo target without losing Nelson mission artifacts or existing local-repo behavior.
constraints: Prefer existing Nelson mission-data and hook patterns.
Do not perform network clones without explicit approval.
Keep backwards compatibility for existing missions.
out_of_scope: Building a full remote execution service.
stop_criteria: Implementation, focused tests, and docs are updated and verified.
handoff_artifacts: Changed files summary, verification commands, Nelson mission artifacts.

amendments.reason: Reconnaissance and user clarification reframed the target mechanism from default gh clone under /tmp to provider-created worktrees from existing local source repositories.
amendments.outcome: Add repository-target support so Nelson missions can run ships in provider-created worktrees from already-existing local source repositories while keeping mission state in one Nelson control repo.
amendments.success_metric: Tests and documentation demonstrate that a mission can record a target source repo, create or use a wt/existing target worktree on branch nelson/<mission-id>, send ships to that working directory, and preserve existing local-repo behavior.
constraints_added: Use an already-existing local source repository as the default target input.
Prefer the user's wt provider for worktree creation when available.
Record the actual provider-created working directory; do not assume one fixed worktree layout.

target_repository_contract.working_dir_policy: capture actual provider-created path or accept explicit existing checkout path
target_repository_contract.multi_repo_policy: explicit only; prefer one ship per target repository
